data.title: US FAA says it met goal of hiring 2,000 controllers this year
data.description:
Washington, Sept 19 (Reuters) - A senior official at the Federal Aviation Administration said Friday the agency met its goal of hiring 2,000 air traffic controllers in the 12 months ending Sept. 30 and aims to hire 2,200 next year.

Deputy FAA Administrator Chris Rocheleau announced the hiring figures at an industry conference.

A persistent shortage of controllers has delayed flights and many are working mandatory overtime and six-day weeks. The FAA is about 3,500 air traffic controllers short of targeted staffing levels. Congress approved $12.5 billion in July to overhaul aging air traffic control systems and boost controller hiring.

(Reporting by David Shepardson)

data.title: Sector Update: Financial
data.description:
(MT Newswires) -- Financial stocks were advancing premarket Friday, with the Financial Select Sector SPDR Fund (XLF) up 0.2%.

The Direxion Daily Financial Bull 3X Shares (FAS) was 0.4% higher and its bearish counterpart Direxion Daily Financial Bear 3X Shares (FAZ) was down 0.5%.

Lloyds Banking (LYG) is selling a significant risk transfer linked to around 500 million British pounds ($682.3 million) of commercial real estate loans, Bloomberg reported, according to people familiar with the matter. Shares of Lloyds Banking Group were down more than 2% pre-bell.

data.title: Oil prices dip as demand fears overshadow Fed rate cut
data.description:
LONDON (Reuters) - Oil prices dropped on Friday as worries over fuel demand outweighed expectations that the year's first interest rate cut by the U.S. Federal Reserve would trigger more consumption. 

Brent crude futures were down 31 cents, or 0.5%, at $67.13 a barrel by 1303 GMT, while U.S. West Texas Intermediate futures lost 41 cents, or 0.6%, to $63.16.

Both benchmarks were still on track for a second consecutive weekly gain.

The Fed cut its policy rate by a quarter of a percentage point on Wednesday and indicated that more cuts would follow as it responded to signs of weakness in the jobs market. 

Lower borrowing costs typically boost demand for oil and push prices higher. 

"The market has been caught between conflicting signals," said Priyanka Sachdeva, an analyst at Phillip Nova.

On the demand side, all energy agencies, including the Energy Information Administration, have signalled concern about weakening demand, tempering expectations of significant near-term price upside, Sachdeva said. 

"On the supply side, planned production increases from OPEC+ and signs of oversupply in U.S. fuel product inventories are weighing on sentiment," she added. 

A higher than expected increase of 4 million barrels to U.S. distillate stockpiles raised worries over demand in the world's top oil consumer and pressured prices. [EIA/S]

The latest economic data also added to concerns, with the U.S. jobs market softening while single-family homebuilding plunged to a multi-year low in August amid a glut of unsold new houses. 

One factor holding back oil prices is an uneven economic recovery, particularly in the U.S., said PVM Oil Associates analyst Tamas Varga.

"The corporate sector is benefiting from ongoing deregulation, whereas consumers are beginning to feel the strain of import tariffs, with both the labour and housing markets showing signs of weakness," he said.

In Russia, finance ministry plans to shield the state budget from oil price fluctuations and Western sanctions eased some supply concerns.

The European Commission will propose banning Russian LNG imports by January 1, 2027, a year earlier than planned, as part of a 19th package of sanctions against Moscow, EU sources said on Friday.

(Reporting by Stephanie Kelly; Additional reporting by Sudarshan Varadhan; Editing by David Goodman and Jan Harvey)

By Stephanie Kelly

data.title: Sector Update: Consumer
data.description:

data.title: Sector Update: Tech
data.description:
(MT Newswires) -- Technology stocks were steady premarket Friday, with the SPDR S&P Semiconductor ETF (XSD) inactive and the Technology Select Sector SPDR Fund (XLK) up a slight 0.2%.

Apple's (AAPL) iPhone 17 sales opened to strong demand for its Pro models, media outlets reported. Shares of Apple were up more than 1% pre-bell.

data.title: Apollo Global Explores Product Partnership With Schroders
data.description:
(MT Newswires) -- Apollo Global Management (APO) is in early discussions with UK-based Schroders to explore a potential product partnership, Bloomberg reported Friday, citing people familiar with the talks.

Talks between the companies' senior management teams are ongoing regarding a private markets partnership under which Apollo could source assets for Schroders, according to the people.

Schroders is also holding talks with other companies to widen its portfolio of offerings, according to the report.

Apollo Global and Schroders didn't immediately reply to MT Newswires' request for comment.

(Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.)

data.title: BHP's Geraldine Slattery Close to Becoming Company's First Female Chief Executive
data.description:
(MT Newswires) -- BHP Group (BHP) is likely to appoint the first female chief executive in its 140-year history, with Geraldine Slattery, head of the company's Australian division, emerging as the lead candidate, the Financial Times said Friday, citing people familiar with the board's thinking.

Current CEO Mike Henry is expected to step down by the middle of 2026, after spending five years in the role, the report said, adding that the BHP's board was "not in a rush" to make a change.

Other contenders for the CEO job include Vandita Pant, the company's chief financial office, chief commercial officer Ragnar Udd, and Brandon Craig, BHP's head of the Americas, according to the report.

BHP did not immediately respond to MT Newswires' request for comment.

(Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.)

data.title: Top Premarket Decliners
data.description:
(MT Newswires) -- Scholastic (SCHL) shares were down 11% in recent Friday premarket activity, a day after the company posted a wider fiscal Q1 loss, excluding one-time items, and lower revenue.

Hess Midstream (HESM) stock was 8% lower before Friday's opening bell after Wells Fargo downgraded it to equalweight from overweight. The company said Thursday that its Q3 net income and adjusted EBITDA are expected to be at the lower end of the previous guidance range.

Orla Mining (ORLA) shares were down 5.1%, continuing Thursday's fall.

NatWest Group (NWG) stock was down 3.6%, a day after the company said in a regulatory filing it bought about ordinary shares from Merrill Lynch International.

Lennar (LEN) stock was down 3.1% after the company reported overnight lower fiscal Q3 adjusted earnings and revenue.

data.title: Nextdecade Insider Bought Shares Worth $686,400, According to a Recent SEC Filing
data.description:
(MT Newswires) -- William C Vrattos, Director, on September 17, 2025, executed a purchase for 100,000 shares in Nextdecade (NEXT) for $686,400. Following the Form 4 filing with the SEC, Vrattos has control over a total of 1,371,019 common shares of the company, with 26,533 shares held directly and 1,344,486 controlled indirectly.

SEC Filing:

https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1612720/000171309825000009/xslF345X05/wk-form4_1758285069.xml

data.title: Newmont Sells Stake in Orla Mining for US$439 Million
data.description:
(MT Newswires) -- Newmont (NGT.TO) said Friday that it completed the sale of its 13.3% stake in Orla Mining (OLA.TO) for US$439 million in proceeds.

Newmont said it divested 43 million shares through TSX facilities at $10.14 each.

The gold miner said the sale is part of its commitment to streamline its equity portfolio and unlock significant cash to support the company's capital allocation.

"While this marks the end of our ownership in Orla, we are confident in their continued ability to drive value for shareholders," Newmont chief executive officer Tom Palmer said.

data.title: Newmont Brief: Announcing Sale of its Interest in Orla Mining
data.description: (MT Newswires) --

data.title: Medtronic Gets US FDA Approval for Altaviva Device to Treat Urge Urinary Incontinence
data.description:
(MT Newswires) -- Medtronic (MDT) said Friday that it has received US Food and Drug Administration approval for the Altaviva device, a minimally invasive implantable, tibial neuromodulation therapy device, which is inserted near the ankle and designed to treat urge urinary incontinence.

The company said the implant procedure does not require sedation or imaging and it is placed slightly below the skin but above the fascia.

Medtronic said it sends electrical impulses to the tibial nerve, helping restore communication between the bladder and the brain to regulate bladder control.

data.title: Exchange-Traded Funds Higher, Equity Futures Mixed Pre-Bell Friday Ahead of Trump, Xi Talks
data.description:
(MT Newswires) -- The broad market exchange-traded fund SPDR S&P 500 ETF Trust (SPY) was up 0.1% and the actively traded Invesco QQQ Trust (QQQ) was 0.2% higher in Friday's premarket activity, ahead of a call between Chinese President Xi Jinping and US President Donald Trump at 9 am ET.

US stock futures were mixed, with S&P 500 Index futures up 0.1%, Dow Jones Industrial Average futures slipping 0.03%, and Nasdaq futures gaining 0.1% before the start of regular trading.

The Baker Hughes domestic oil-and-gas rig count at 1 pm ET.

San Francisco Fed President Mary Daly is scheduled to speak on Friday

In premarket activity, bitcoin was down by 0.9%. Among cryptocurrency ETFs, the cryptocurrency fund ProShares Bitcoin Strategy ETF (BITO) was 0.9% lower, Ether ETF (EETH) was down 1.3%, and Bitcoin & Ether Market Cap Weight ETF (BETH) was up 3%.

Power Play:

Energy

The iShares US Energy ETF (IYE) was inactive, while the Energy Select Sector SPDR Fund (XLE) was flat.

Hess Midstream (HESM) stock was down more than 7% before Friday's opening bell after Wells Fargo downgraded it to equalweight from overweight. The company said Thursday that its Q3 net income and adjusted EBITDA are expected to be at the lower end of the previous guidance range.

Winners and Losers:

Consumer

The Consumer Staples Select Sector SPDR Fund (XLP) was up 0.1%, while the Vanguard Consumer Staples Fund (VDC) was flat. The iShares US Consumer Staples ETF (IYK) was inactive, and the Consumer Discretionary Select Sector SPDR Fund (XLY) advanced 0.3%. The VanEck Retail ETF (RTH) was inactive, while the SPDR S&P Retail ETF (XRT) was 0.5% higher.

Lennar (LEN) shares were down more than 3% pre-bell as the homebuilder issued a fiscal Q4 delivery outlook below market estimates.

Industrial

Industrial Select Sector SPDR Fund (XLI) advanced 0.1% while the Vanguard Industrials Index Fund (VIS) and the iShares US Industrials ETF (IYJ) were inactive.

FedEx (FDX) shares were up 3% in recent premarket activity after the company's fiscal Q1 results rose more than expected.

Health Care

The Health Care Select Sector SPDR Fund (XLV) retreated 0.03%. The Vanguard Health Care Index Fund (VHT) advanced 0.02%, while the iShares US Healthcare ETF (IYH) and the iShares Biotechnology ETF (IBB) were inactive.

Syndax Pharmaceuticals (SNDX) stock was up more than 2% premarket after the company said the National Comprehensive Cancer Network included its blood cancer drug Revuforj, or revumenib, as a category 2A recommendation in the Clinical Practice Guidelines in Oncology for relapsed or refractory acute myeloid leukemia with an NPM1 mutation, an aggressive blood cancer associated with high rates of relapse.

Financial

Financial Select Sector SPDR Fund (XLF) advanced 0.1%. Direxion Daily Financial Bull 3X Shares (FAS) was up 0.2%, while its bearish counterpart Direxion Daily Financial Bear 3X Shares (FAZ) was flat.

Lloyds Banking (LYG) shares were down more than 2% pre-bell Friday after Bloomberg News reported the bank is selling a significant risk transfer linked to around 500 million British pounds ($682.3 million) of commercial real estate loans.

Technology

Technology Select Sector SPDR Fund (XLK) gained 0.1%, and the iShares US Technology ETF (IYW) retreated 0.01%, while the iShares Expanded Tech Sector ETF (IGM) was up 0.8%. Among semiconductor ETFs, SPDR S&P Semiconductor ETF (XSD) was inactive, while the iShares Semiconductor ETF (SOXX) rose by 0.4%.

WeRide (WRD) shares were up more than 1% in recent premarket activity after the company said its Robobus received a test permit in Belgium for a level 4 autonomous shuttle, allowing it to test on public roads.

Commodities

Front-month US West Texas Intermediate crude oil was down 0.3% at $63.38 per barrel on the New York Mercantile Exchange. Natural gas retreated 0.6% to $2.92 per 1 million British Thermal Units. The United States Oil Fund (USO) was 0.5% lower, while the United States Natural Gas Fund (UNG) declined by 0.2%.

Gold futures for December were up by 0.1% at $3,680.20 an ounce on the Comex, while silver futures gained 0.5% to reach $42.31 an ounce. SPDR Gold Shares (GLD) was up 0.1%, and the iShares Silver Trust (SLV) was 0.7% higher.

data.title: Rio Tinto Approached by Glencore for Congolese Copper Mine Stake Sale
data.description:
(MT Newswires) -- Rio Tinto (RIO) and other companies have received informal notice from Glencore that it is open to selling a stake in its copper mine in Congo, Bloomberg News reported Friday, citing people familiar with the matter.

Glencore's assets in the Congo include Kamoto Copper, a partnership with Gecamines and the Congolese government.

The report said that Glencore has held talks with potential bidders for the mine, including Orion Resource Partners, which had previously approached the London-listed miner with a proposal for the New York fund to buy the Congolese project through its joint venture with Abu Dhabi's ADQ.

A potential sale of the mine has been discussed as part of talks between the US and Congo over a minerals and infrastructure partnership, the report added.

Glencore and Orion Resource Partners declined to comment, while Rio Tinto did not immediately respond to request for comment from MT Newswires.

(Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.)

data.title: Blackstone appoints Katie Keenan as CEO of real estate income trust
data.description:
(Reuters) - Blackstone said on Friday it has appointed Katie Keenan as the CEO of its real estate income trust and global head of the Core+ business.

Keenan has served in several leadership roles at the firm in her tenure of over a decade. She currently serves as the global co-chief investment officer of Blackstone's real estate debt strategies and CEO of mortgage trust.

She succeeds Wesley LePatner in both roles after her tragic death. LePatner, a senior executive at Blackstone, was gunned down in July when a gunman opened fire as she was leaving her Midtown Manhattan office.

LePatner's death was at random. Neither she nor Blackstone was specifically targeted.

Blackstone also named Zaneta Koplewicz as the co-president and director of the real estate income trust.

(Reporting by Pritam Biswas in Bengaluru; Editing by Leroy Leo)

data.title: Equinix Opens Chennai, India Data Center With Initial $69 Million Investment
data.description:
(MT Newswires) -- Equinix (EQIX) said Friday that it is opening a data center in Chennai, India with an initial investment of $69 million.

The so-called CN1 facility, located on a nearly six-acre plot of land, will be connected to Equinix's Mumbai campus and offer services to both local and international businesses, the company said.

The four-story data center will begin with 800 cabinets and eventually support 4,250 cabinets, with support enabled for liquid cooling technology, the company said.

Shares of Equinix were up 1.7% in recent Friday premarket activity.

data.title: Skye Bioscience Says Liver Disease Drug Candidate Safe, Well Tolerated in Phase 1B Clinical Trial
data.description:
(MT Newswires) -- Skye Bioscience (SKYE) said Friday that results from the phase 1b study of nimacimab in subjects with metabolic-associated steatotic liver disease, or MASLD, demonstrated that the drug candidate was safe and well-tolerated.

Results also indicated that nimacimab has "predictable pharmacokinetics and low immunogenicity," according to the company. The company also said that the results showed no serious adverse events, and no evidence of neuropsychiatric safety signals.

data.title: Russia's ultra low-sulphur diesel exports from Primorsk scheduled to fall in September, traders say
data.description:
Moscow, Sept 19 (Reuters) - Russia's September exports of ultra low-sulphur diesel from the Baltic Sea port of Primorsk are scheduled to fall by 14.5% from August to 1.1 million metric tons, two traders said on Friday.

However, actual loadings could be lower this month as several Russian refineries have been hit by drone attacks, including Surgutneftegaz's Kirishinefteorgsintez refinery, market sources added.

According to traders and LSEG shipping data, diesel loading from the Primorsk port has totalled around 625,000 tons in September so far.

(Reporting by Reuters; Editing by Jan Harvey)

data.title: China Energy Storage Purchases Property-Holding Firm
data.description:
(MT Newswires) -- China Energy Storage Technology Development (HKG:1143) completed the acquisition of Circuit Development for HK$20.8 million, a Friday Hong Kong bourse filing said.

Following certain adjustments, the final consideration for the purchase is over HK$21.1 million, which has been settled by the electronic manufacturing services provider in cash.

Circuit Development is the holder of a property in Hong Kong's New Territories where China Energy Storage has an office.

data.title: Winshine Science Raises HK$10.3 Million from Share Placement
data.description:
(MT Newswires) -- Winshine Science (HKG:0209) completed the placing of nearly 31 million shares to at least six investors at HK$0.35 apiece to raise net proceeds of a little over HK$10.3 million, a Friday Hong Kong bourse filing said.

The shares represent roughly 16.67% of the security investment and the toy manufacturing company's enlarged issued share capital.

Proceeds will be used to repay loans, settle outstanding audit and professional fees, and for working capital and general corporate purposes.

data.title: Roche's Subcutaneous Lunsumio for Lymphoma Gets EU Medicines Agency Panel Backing
data.description:
(MT Newswires) -- Roche (ROG.SW, RO.SW) said Friday a European Medicines Agency committee recommended the approval of Lunsumio's subcutaneous formula in treating follicular lymphoma, a type of blood cancer.

The EMA's Committee for Medicinal Products for Human Use backed the use of subcutaneous Lunsumio for adults with relapsed or refractory follicular lymphoma after two or more lines of systemic therapy. The European Commission is expected to issue a final decision on the therapy's marketing authorization soon.

The positive opinion was based on the phase 2 GO29781 trial, in which subcutaneous Lunsumio worked as well as the intravenous version, with no unexpected safety issues.

data.title: Swedish Competition Authority May Block Telia's Acquisition of Bredband2, Reports Telekomnyheterna
data.description:
Telecom operator Telia's planned acquisition of Bredband2 could be blocked by the Swedish Competition Authority, according to industry newsletter Telekomnyheterna, as highlighted by Di on Friday.

In its article, Telekomnyheterna notes that Telia, in its efforts to convince the authority, has referenced a draft for new fiber regulation from the Swedish Post and Telecom Authority (PTS)--a draft that has come under scrutiny. Among others, the Competition Authority itself is said to have questioned the proposal.

Telekomnyheterna further reports that if the Competition Authority assesses Telia's share of the fiber market from a national perspective, it would amount to a significant 47.6 percent.

Telia's Head of Communications, Tobias Gyhlénius, told Di that the market is fragmented and that the deal would benefit consumers rather than exclude competitors.

“Since these are the most important aspects from a regulatory perspective, we believe that the relevant approvals will be granted,” he stated.

data.title: Digital Domain Names CEO as Chair
data.description:
(MT Newswires) -- Digital Domain (HKG:0547) named CEO Wong Cheung Lok as its new chairman effective Sept. 30, a Friday Hong Kong bourse filing said.

The move came after acting chairman, Hooi Hing Lee, resigned due to other work commitments effective the same date.

Hooi Hing Lee will remain as the visual effects studios company's chief strategic advisor.

data.title: New Century Group Unit Extends HK$30.2 Million Loan
data.description:
(MT Newswires) -- New Century Group Hong Kong (HKG:0234) unit ETC Finance agreed to grant a mortgage loan worth a total of HK$30.2 million to three borrowers for a 12-month term, a Friday Hong Kong bourse filing said.

The loan, granted to Y. K. Audio, Chan Ying Kwong, and Chan Tak Wai, Daisy, carries an 11% per annum interest rate.

Proceeds will be used to fully settle a previous loan of the same amount obtained by the borrowers from the money lender.

A first legal charge over multiple properties was pledged as security against the loan.

data.title: Redsun Properties Further Extends RSA Fee Deadlines
data.description:
(MT Newswires) -- Redsun Properties Group (HKG:1996) further extended the early fee deadline under its restructuring support agreement (RSA) to Oct. 3 from Sept. 19, a Friday Hong Kong bourse filing said.

The base fee deadline was also postponed to Oct. 17 from Oct. 3.

The move comes about as certain creditors need additional time to complete their internal processes to accede to the agreement.

data.title: Roche's Subcutaneous Lunsumio for Lymphoma Treatment Gets EU Medicines Agency Panel Backing
data.description: (MT Newswires) --

data.title: US SEC chair: Market can decide 'proper cadence' of company reports
data.description:
Washington, Sept 19 (Reuters) - Investors and banks are likely to help set any new timeline for public company reporting as regulators press ahead on President Donald Trump's bid to end quarterly reports, US Securities and Exchange Commission Chairman Paul Atkins told CNBC on Friday.

(Reporting by Susan Heavey)

data.title: CHMP gives positive opinion on new formulation of Roche's Lunsumio
data.description:
On Friday, Roche announced that it had received a positive opinion from the Committee for Medicinal Products for Human Use (CHMP) of the European Medicines Agency (EMA) regarding the marketing authorization application for the subcutaneous formulation of its new treatment for relapsed or refractory follicular lymphoma.

The group specifies that the CHMP's recommendation concerns the subcutaneous administration of Lunsumio in the treatment of patients with relapsed or refractory (R/R) follicular lymphoma (FL) who have received at least two prior systemic therapies.

Lunsumio, a bispecific antibody that releases cytotoxic proteins into lymphocytes, represents a new type of immunotherapy, i.e., a therapeutic option without chemotherapy.

In a press release, the Swiss pharmaceutical company added that marketing authorization from the European Commission is expected "in the near future."

data.title: Angola to decide on $1 billion JPMorgan deal by November, finance official says
data.description:
LONDON (Reuters) - Angola will decide by November whether to roll over its $1 billion total return swap deal with JPMorgan, or potentially raise the money on international capital markets, a senior debt official told Reuters.

JPMorgan and Angola agreed in December a $1 billion, one-year derivative contract known as a total return swap, backed by $1.9 billion in government dollar bonds, which will expire at the end of this year. 

"We have some options," Dorivaldo Teixeira, General Director of the Public Debt Management Unit at Angola's finance ministry, told Reuters on the sidelines of investor meetings in London.

If market conditions were right, Angola could issue debt to raise the funds, repay partially, or extend the current arrangement.     

"It depends on the cost," he said. While he said the market conditions for smaller, riskier issuers were improving, with yields "going in the right direction", he noted that the JPMorgan facility cost was lower than the country's Eurobonds and "if I can extend it, probably I will use it." 

CONTAINING THE COSTS

The yield on Angola's international bonds currently stands at roughly 10%, according to JPMorgan EMBI data.

Still, he said Angola would push for a better deal than the current 9% it was paying on the arrangement, whether that was from the bank or from markets. The full terms of the total return swap have never been published.

The deal grabbed headlines in April, when Angola had to pay $200 million to JPMorgan as additional security for its collateralised bond in a margin call after U.S. tariff turmoil pushed oil prices - and Angola's bonds - sharply lower.

In November, the country also has to pay back just over $860 million it has outstanding on a dollar-denominated bond it sold in 2015.

Finance officials are also working to increase transparency by publishing debt statistics more regularly, Teixeira said. The finance ministry has started issuing its debt bulletin quarterly, and it is aiming to publish monthly starting next year, while ensuring key information and statistics are available in English as well as Portuguese.

"The perception of risk of Angola was heightened a little bit because we didn't communicate as much. People need more information about what's going on," Teixeira said in an interview late on Thursday, adding he hoped this would help lower Angola's borrowing costs.

CAUTIOUS ON CRUDE

Teixeira said finance officials are pressing for a more conservative oil price assumption in the 2026 budget after the government had to stress-test its 2025 spending due to a drop in prices below their $70 per barrel assumption.

Brent crude is trading at $67, and Teixeira said the final budget deficit figures could be higher than expected due to the fall in revenue.

"One of the lessons that we learned from this, from this process, is probably next year, we should take a little bit more conservative approach that will help us to execute the budget in the most seamless way."

Teixeira declined to peg a specific price but said officials base their assumptions on top analyst forecasts.

A Reuters poll showed most analysts expect prices to drift down next year, with Brent forecast at $62.98 in the second quarter of 2026.

(Reporting by Karin Strohecker and Libby George, editing by Amanda Cooper and Hugh Lawson)

By Karin Strohecker and Libby George

data.title: Pirc advises Wise shareholders to oppose resolution on share repurchase authorisation
data.description:
Sept 19 (Reuters) - Pirc:

  • Proxy advisory firm Pirc recommends Wise PLC shareholders oppose vote on resolution to authorise share repurchase
  • Proxy advisory firm Pirc recommends Wise PLC shareholders abstain vote on resolution to approve remuneration report
  • Pirc recommends Kainos Group shareholders to abstain voting on resolutions to approve remuneration report & remuneration policy at AGM

data.title: S&P says Malaysia 'A-/A-2' foreign currency & 'A/A-1' local currency ratings affirmed outlook stable
data.description:
Sept 19 (Reuters) - S&P Global:

* S&P says Malaysia 'A-/A-2' foreign currency and 'A/A-1' local currency ratings affirmed; outlook stable

data.title: Rolls-Royce to lead EU's new clean aviation project unified to transform & decarbonise aviation
data.description:
Sept 19 (Reuters) - Rolls-Royce Holdings PLC:

  • Rolls-Royce: to lead EU's new clean aviation project unified to transform and decarbonise aviation
  • Rolls-Royce: initiatives including new aircraft concepts & innovative propulsion technologies to receive funding of about €945 million - website

data.title: Sabrina Soussan set to take over as chair of Continental's supervisory board
data.description:
Continental announces that Sabrina Soussan is expected to join the supervisory board at the end of September 2025 as a shareholder representative. She will be nominated for election at the AGM on April 30, 2026, and is then expected to succeed Wolfgang Reitzle as chair of the board.

The company states that several members are leaving the board following the spin-off of Aumovio, including Stefan E. Buchner, Gunter Dunkel, Christiane Benner, and Matthias Ebenau. The local court is to appoint four new employee representatives: Petra Hartwig, Sabine Kühn, Michael Linnartz, and Nicole Werner.

Reitzle hails a "highly qualified candidate," while Soussan says she is "honored" to join the board. A Franco-German executive, she has more than 25 years of experience in the automotive industry and recently headed the SUEZ group.

data.title: Aviva completes £113m buy-in deals with Fenwick's pension schemes
data.description:
Sept 19 (Reuters) - Aviva PLC:

  • Aviva completes £113m buy-in deals with Fenwick's pension schemes
